guide.title: Bill Creation Guide
guide.subtitle: Pick a topic on the left to read the steps

pages.title: Billing system overview
pages.html:
The PPAde billing system supports two main bill types:

  • Manual — pick a date range + rate yourself, generate immediately
  • Auto — set a cutoff day, the system bills automatically every month

Calculations support both single value (1 meter × 1 field) and formulas (e.g. meter A − meter B), with optional extra columns in the bill table.

Where to start?

Open Plant detail → click the Billing tab → you'll see the toggle "Manual" / "Auto Schedule".

pages.title: Permissions
pages.html:
To create/edit/delete bills, the user must have access to the Plant and the right role:

ActionSuperadminAdmin
View bills✅✅ (only assigned plants)
Create/edit manual bill✅✅
Create/edit auto schedule✅✅
Delete bill✅✅
Delete auto schedule (reset)✅❌

Every action is logged in History Edit automatically.

pages.title: Step 1 — Open the modal
pages.html:
1. Click the "Manual" tab in the Billing strip
  2. Hit the "+ New Bill" button at the top right
  3. The "Create electricity bill" modal will open

pages.title: Step 2 — Set the period
pages.html:
Fill in the first section:

  • Start date — first day of the period (e.g. 1 May 2026)
  • End date — last day (e.g. 31 May 2026)
Limit

Bills can span at most 60 days. Longer ranges are rejected with a warning.

pages.title: Step 3 — Rate and type
pages.html:
• Rate (฿/kWh) — price per unit (3 decimal places)
  • Rate type — "Flat rate" (single rate) or "TOU" (Time of Use)

pages.title: Step 4 — Calculation mode
pages.html:
Pick one of two modes:

  • Single value — pick 1 meter + 1 field
  • Formula — build a 2-term formula like meter-A.energy_in − meter-B.energy_out

Click the reset (circular arrow) icon to clear and start over.

pages.title: Step 5 — Confirm
pages.html:
1. Review all the inputs
  2. Click "Create Bill"
  3. The button shows a "Creating bill..." spinner while it pulls daily data from the API
  4. An alert reports the bill number + total amount
  5. The bill appears in the history table below
If an error occurs the system will alert — check network/permissions and retry.

pages.title: What is an Auto Schedule?
pages.html:
An Auto Schedule is a predefined billing cycle. The system creates the bill automatically when each month's cutoff day arrives.

  • Set a cutoff day 1–31 (e.g. 5 = bill on the 5th of every month)
  • One plant can have multiple cycles (e.g. day 5 + day 25)
  • On the cutoff day → the system fetches data + captures live meter totalizers + creates the bill + saves to history

pages.title: Adding a schedule
pages.html:
1. Pick the "Auto Schedule" tab
  2. Click "Configure auto"
  3. Set cutoff day, rate, calculation mode, meters
  4. Hit "Save auto"
  5. The cycle appears in "Configured Auto Bill Schedules"

pages.title: Preview / generate now
pages.html:
Each schedule row exposes these actions:

  • "Preview" — preview the most recent full month (not committed)
  • "Preview current" — partial preview from cycle start to today
  • "Create bill" — bill immediately with what's available so far, persists to history

pages.title: Edit / delete
pages.html:
• Edit — open the modal and change anything
  • Delete — Superadmin only (Admin can't)
  • Every change is recorded in History Edit

pages.title: Before / after meter readings
pages.html:
Only auto bills capture cumulative meter readings (Live API totalizer):

  • "After" = current kWh from the API at bill creation
  • "Before" = the previous auto bill's "After" (same plant + meter)
  • The first bill of a meter shows "—" for "Before" (no prior reading)

Rendered as a small per-meter card on the bill header (preview + PDF).

pages.title: Two-term formulas
pages.html:
The "Formula" mode builds an expression from two meters joined by an operator + − × ÷.

Example: Solar.energy_in − MDB.energy_out = remaining net energy

  • Pick meter A + field A
  • Pick operator (+, −, ×, ÷)
  • Pick meter B + field B
  • Set the "result name" shown on the bill

pages.title: Formula Columns — extra columns
pages.html:
The bill table normally shows one total column (from the main formula), but you can add more:

  1. Click "+ Add column" — add a basic column (meter × field)
  2. Click "+ Add calc column" — add a column with a formula (like the basic 2-term)
  3. Click "Remove column" — drop the most recent column

Each column has an include checkbox — checked = counted toward the total, unchecked = shown but not counted.

pages.title: Use cases
pages.html:
• Solar + grid: columns Solar / MDB In / MDB Out / Net (excluded)
  • Multi-tenant meters: columns tenant 1 / tenant 2 / tenant 3 — sum total
  • Discounts: raw column + discount column (excluded)

pages.title: Preview a bill
pages.html:
Click "Preview" on a bill row in history.

  • Opens an A4-sized modal — paginated by daily rows
  • Header shows company info + period details
  • Auto bills include the per-meter before/after reading cards
  • Daily table + totals
  • Final summary + amount

pages.title: Download PDF
pages.html:
1. Open the bill preview
  2. Click "Download PDF"
  3. The system uses the browser's window.print() → choose "Save as PDF"
  4. Set paper size: A4, margins: None

The PDF preserves table colors and the meter reading cards as they appear in preview.

pages.title: Every action is logged
pages.html:
The "History Edit" tab on the Plant detail page shows an audit log of every change:

  • Bill created (manual + auto) — green
  • Bill deleted — red
  • Schedule added/updated/removed — green / amber / red

Each entry: avatar + name + role + details + timestamp (relative + absolute).

pages.title: Filters
pages.html:
• Type: All / Bills / Schedules
  • Date: pick a date in the picker → only entries from that day
  • Click the 🔄 button to reload the latest from the server

pages.title: Who sees the log
pages.html:
The audit log is shared per plant — anyone who can access the Plant sees the full log of that Plant (no actor-based filter).

The actor info comes from the server session — it cannot be spoofed from the client.

Up to 500 entries are kept per plant — older ones are trimmed.
